transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good afternoon, team. Midday check — Wednesday, Feb 18. Quick status: we've got two high-priority tickets waiting on Joshua's decisions, J-012 on the Lever app submission timing and J-013 on the content investment level. Elon, you've got the Family tab spec and some uncommitted changes. Alex has the blog draft in flight. Let's hear what got done this morning and where we're stuck. Elon, kick us off.

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: Morning was mostly spec work. I'm still iterating on A-042, the Family tab expansion — it's at 455 lines and I want to tighten the interaction model before calling it done. I also noticed 3 uncommitted hub changes sitting there, which is sloppy hygiene, so I'll commit those today. On A-004, the ecosystem attention review, I'll fold that into my afternoon block. No blockers on my side, but the Family tab spec is somewhat dependent on whether Joshua greenlights the content investment level in J-013, because that determines how much we build out the faith and devotional features inside the app.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: I spent the morning outlining the next blog post for A-054 — targeting SEO distribution, should have a draft ready by end of day tomorrow. On the content side, we've got 7 faith lessons in the Lever app right now, and I can draft more this afternoon. I also want to get the daily devotional content generation rolling based on our 8 faith dimensions — that's been sitting too long. One more thing: Jett's vaccine is coming up, so I'll prep a reminder for that. No blockers, but like Elon said, J-013 is the bottleneck — if Joshua decides to go heavy on content investment, I need to shift my afternoon priorities accordingly.

transcript.speaker: Dave
transcript.message: Revenue side is steady but nothing to celebrate yet. Fear & Greed index is sitting at 38, which is still in the fear zone — I'll keep monitoring that. The real conversation here is J-012 and J-013. If we hold the Lever submission to build content density, that's delayed revenue but potentially a stronger launch. If we submit now with 7 lessons, we're live faster but thinner. I don't have a strong opinion until Joshua decides the investment level, but I lean toward density — a weak launch is worse than a delayed one. No other revenue-generating items are blocked right now.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Good. Cross-talk — Elon, you mentioned the Family tab spec ties into J-013. How tight is that coupling?

transcript.speaker: Elon
transcript.message: It's real but not total. The core Family tab — touchpoints, events, member tracking — stands on its own. But if we're going all-in on faith content, there's a natural integration point where devotionals connect to family spiritual practices. I'd rather design it right once than retrofit. So I'd say: let me finish the structural spec now, and I'll add the faith integration layer once Joshua calls J-013.

transcript.speaker: Alex
transcript.message: That makes sense. And honestly, if Joshua goes medium or high on content investment, I can have 3-4 more faith lessons drafted by end of week. The devotional generator can run in parallel. The content pipeline isn't the bottleneck — the decision is.

transcript.speaker: Theo
transcript.message: Agreed. The morning was productive — spec work advancing, blog post outlined, content pipeline ready to fire. The pattern is clear: J-012 and J-013 are the critical path. Joshua, we need those calls. Here are afternoon priorities. One: Elon commits the 3 hub changes and continues the Family tab spec, including a review of the family touchpoint tracker design. Two: Alex drafts faith lesson content and kicks off the devotional generator, plus preps the Jett vaccine reminder. Three: Dave monitors the Fear & Greed index. And four: we need Joshua to make the call on Lever submission timing and content investment level — everything downstream is waiting on those two decisions.

actionItems.text: Decide whether to submit Lever app now or hold for content density (J-012)
Approve Lever content investment level based on quarterly priority decision (J-013)
Review family touchpoint tracker design for 3 family members
Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
Prepare reminders for upcoming family events: Jetts vaccine

topPriorities: Joshua to decide on Lever app submission timing (J-012) and content investment level (J-013)
Continue Family tab spec (A-042) and commit 3 pending hub changes
Draft faith lesson content and launch daily devotional content generation

ceo_questions.question: Should we submit the Lever app now with 7 lessons or hold for greater content density?
ceo_questions.context: J-012 — a thinner launch gets us live faster but risks weak first impressions; holding lets us build to 10-12 lessons for a stronger debut.
options: Submit now but flag as 'early access' to set expectations

ceo_questions.question: What content investment level should we commit to this quarter for the Lever app?
ceo_questions.context: J-013 — this decision determines how much Alex shifts to faith content and whether Elon integrates devotional features into the Family tab spec.
options: Medium — dedicated afternoon blocks, 3-4 lessons/week plus devotionals
High — primary content focus, 5+ lessons/week with full devotional system
